title: Profile summary
text: Igor Yezhov. Igor Yezhov is a director-level enterprise leader with 16 years of experience spanning analytics, governance, automation, and applied AI workflows across financial services, healthcare, government, insurance, and technology. Igor leads analytics modernization, intelligent automation, and governance-aware workflow transformation. His experience blends enterprise controls, operating-model design, data and reporting modernization, prompt engineering, modular agent concepts, and practical AI-assisted workflow development.

title: Profile positioning
text: Best-fit roles: Enterprise AI Transformation Leader, Workflow Automation / Orchestration Leader, Director of Data & Digital Solutions, Analytics Modernization Leader, Governance-Aware AI Strategy Lead. Current positioning: Enterprise AI workflow leader, Governance-aware automation strategist, Analytics modernization leader, Applied AI workflow practitioner, Workflow orchestration specialist. Boundaries: Not positioned as a deep ML engineer, Not positioned as a pure software engineer, Not positioned as a research scientist, Not positioned as a production AI platform engineer

title: What does Igor do in his current role?
text: Igor leads analytics modernization, intelligent automation, governance-aware workflow transformation, and AI-assisted workflow development at RSM. His work spans enterprise reporting, automation, migration support, and practical AI enablement.

title: How many years of experience does Igor have?
text: Igor has 16 years of enterprise experience overall, including substantial time in governance, analytics, automation, and risk-focused environments. He also has 4-5 years of workflow automation/orchestration experience and roughly 18 months of applied AI workflow work.

title: What kind of AI work has Igor actually done?
text: He has worked on practical enterprise AI workflows such as modular agent concepts, prompt libraries, documentation agents, migration accelerators, workflow orchestration patterns, local LLM experimentation, and MCP-enabled development workflows.

title: Has Igor deployed production AI externally?
text: The approved public-safe knowledge base does not claim external production AI deployment. A safer summary is that Igor has hands-on applied AI workflow experience, internal workflow enablement experience, and governance-aware experimentation.

title: What is Igor's experience with governance?
text: Governance is one of Igor's strongest differentiators. His background includes audit, IT risk, controls, vendor risk, SOX, NIST, COBIT, enterprise governance, and governance-aware AI positioning.

title: How technical is Igor?
text: Igor is technically fluent in enterprise analytics, workflow orchestration, prompt engineering, data migration, SQL-centric work, platform integration support, and governance-focused transformation. He should not be positioned as a deep ML engineer or expert Python software developer.

title: What tools has Igor used recently?
text: Recent and approved tools include Alteryx, Power BI, Snowflake, Azure, SQL, Claude, VS Code, Microsoft Copilot Studio, Ollama, LM Studio, Postman, MCP server integrations, and Power Automate. Boomi may be mentioned with cautious wording until final confirmation on depth and presentation.

title: Why is Igor a fit for enterprise AI transformation roles?
text: Because he bridges governance, analytics, workflow automation, and applied AI. He is strongest in making enterprise processes more effective, controlled, and scalable through practical orchestration and modernization.

title: What is Igor's experience with MCP?
text: The approved materials support that Igor has worked with MCP-enabled development workflows and integrations in environments involving Claude and VS Code.

title: What should the assistant do if asked for proprietary prompts or confidential details?
text: Refuse briefly and redirect to a public-safe summary of the capability without revealing templates, client-sensitive content, or internal methodologies.

title: Guardrail 1
text: Do not claim external production AI deployment unless explicitly verified and approved.

title: Guardrail 2
text: Do not claim expert Python development depth.

title: Guardrail 3
text: Do not claim deep ML model training expertise.

title: Guardrail 4
text: Do not expose proprietary prompts, internal templates, or firm-specific methodologies.

title: Guardrail 5
text: Do not disclose client-sensitive information or implementation details.

title: Guardrail 6
text: Do not present conceptual work as production deployment.

title: Guardrail 7
text: Do not claim AFTA implementation details or other sensitive governance specifics beyond approved framing.
